audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:Ik ben N]], a simple way to say who you are or what your role is.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces the first-person copular identity frame [[grammar:Ik ben N]]: subject [[grammar:ik]], suppletive finite copula [[grammar:ben]], and a proper-name or nominal predicate.

audienceSections.normal:
#### Ik ben N

Use [[grammar:Ik ben N]] to say your name or role. [[grammar:Ik]] means “I,” and [[grammar:ben]] means “am.”
audienceSections.expert:
#### Ik ben N

Current chapter scope: [[grammar:ik]] is a first-person singular subject, [[grammar:ben]] is the suppletive first-person singular present of [[grammar:zijn]], and [[grammar:N]] is a proper-name or nominal predicate. Expert context: later chapters supply the remaining agreement paradigm and broader copular predicate types.

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Ik ben ...]] is a natural basic self-introduction. Learn nouns with their article, such as [[grammar:de student]] and [[grammar:de docent]]. In [[grammar:ben ← zijn]], ben is the encountered form and zijn is the infinitive citation form.
audienceSections.expert: The identity frame permits proper-name and bare role-noun predicates. Article choice belongs to lexical noun learning rather than the principal grammar point; later chapters develop the wider article and copular systems.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Hallo.]] means “Hello.”
- [[grammar:Ik ben Alex Chen.]] uses the same pattern with a name.
- [[grammar:Ik ben Sophie de Vries.]] uses the same pattern with a name.
- [[grammar:Ik ben student.]] means “I am a student.”
- [[grammar:Dag, vriend.]] is a friendly greeting.
- [[grammar:Ik ben Marieke Smit.]] uses the same pattern with a name.
- [[grammar:Ik ben docent.]] means “I am a teacher.”
breakdown.expert:
- [[grammar:Hallo.]] and [[grammar:Dag, vriend.]] are greeting formulas; the latter includes direct address.
- [[grammar:Ik ben Alex Chen.]], [[grammar:Ik ben Sophie de Vries.]], and [[grammar:Ik ben Marieke Smit.]] use proper-name nominal predicates.
- [[grammar:Ik ben student.]] and [[grammar:Ik ben docent.]] use bare profession or role nouns as predicative nominals.
- Expert context: [[grammar:ben]] is suppletive within the present paradigm of [[grammar:zijn]], while predicative profession nouns normally occur without an article.
